accreditation.title: Ministry Inspected Private School
accreditation.statement: EMCS is inspected by the Ontario Ministry of Education and authorized to grant credits toward the Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD).
accreditation.details: Our credits hold the same value as those earned in traditional Ontario day schools and are recognized by all Canadian colleges and universities. Transcripts are sent directly to OUAC, OCAS, and educational institutions worldwide.

about.title: About EMCS
about.subtitle: Excellence Maple Canadian School

whoWeAre.title: Who We Are
whoWeAre.content: Excellence Maple Canadian School (EMCS), operating as Toronto Academy of English, Mathematics and Coding (EMC), is a Ministry-inspected private school authorized by the Ontario Ministry of Education to grant OSSD credits. We provide high-quality education for students from Grades 1-12 based on the official Ontario Curriculum. As an IB World School for the Diploma Programme, we maintain the highest standards in curriculum delivery and student support.
whoWeAre.bsid: BSID: 665588 | Inspected by the Ontario Ministry of Education

about.storyText: EMCS was founded with a mission to bring world-class Canadian education to students around the globe. We recognized that geography should never be a barrier to quality education. Since our founding, we have helped thousands of students earn Ontario high school credits, complete their OSSD, and gain admission to top universities worldwide.
about.missionText: To provide flexible, high-quality Ontario curriculum education that empowers students to achieve their academic goals and gain admission to top universities worldwide. We strive to inspire and equip our students with the necessary mindset and skills to become independent lifelong learners, fully dedicated to making tangible and positive contributions to their communities and beyond.

philosophy.title: Our Teaching Philosophy
philosophy.content: Led by our motto, 'Teach for love. Learn for life', we are dedicated to fostering a dynamic community of individuals committed to lifelong learning. We embody the fundamental values and principles that prepare students for success in higher education and beyond.

about.accreditationText: We are a Ministry-inspected private school authorized by the Ontario Ministry of Education to grant Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD) credits.

accreditationDetails.creditDetails: Same as traditional Ontario day schools

whatThisMeans.title: What This Means
whatThisMeans.point1: Our OSSD is the exact same diploma issued by traditional Ontario day schools
whatThisMeans.point2: Credits are recognized by all Canadian colleges and universities
whatThisMeans.point3: International students can earn Canadian credentials without a study permit
whatThisMeans.point4: Marks are sent directly to OUAC and OCAS for post-secondary applications

team.title: Leadership Team
team.subtitle: Dedicated educators committed to your success

excellence.title: Excellence
excellence.desc: We maintain the highest standards in curriculum delivery, assessment, and student support.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.desc: Quality education should be available to all students, regardless of location or circumstances.

innovation.title: Innovation
innovation.desc: We leverage technology to create engaging and effective learning experiences that prepare students for the future.

support.title: Support
support.desc: Every student receives personalized guidance throughout their educational journey.

missionStatement.title: Our Mission
missionStatement.content: Toronto Academy of EMC strives to inspire and equip our students with the necessary mindset and skills to become independent lifelong learners, fully dedicated to making tangible and positive contributions to their communities and beyond.

principalMessage.title: Principal's Message
principalMessage.content: As the School year comes to an end, I want to extend a warm gratitude to our students, faculty and staff for another wonderful year of learning and growth. Every day I have the privilege of watching our students overcome new challenges and persevere, as they strive towards their academic goals and career aspirations.

overview.title: Program Overview
overview.content: This program supports students in Grades 1-5 in building essential academic foundations aligned with the Ontario Curriculum. Core subjects include English (Language), Mathematics, Science, Social Studies, The Arts, and Health & Physical Education.
overview.french: French as a Second Language is introduced starting in Grade 4.
overview.purpose: Prepare students for future studies within the Ontario education system through a clear, structured, and academically sound learning pathway. Students study all subjects in English, supporting language development and early exposure to an international learning environment.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our programs
faq.a1: Yes, EMCS is a Ministry-inspected private school authorized by the Ontario Ministry of Education to grant OSSD credits. Our BSID is 665588. Our credits hold the same value as those earned in traditional Ontario day schools.
faq.a2: We have continuous enrollment year-round. You can register at any time and typically start your course within 24-48 hours.
faq.q3: How long do I have to complete a course?
faq.a3: You have up to 12 months from enrollment to complete each course, though most students finish in 3-4 months. Fast-track options allow completion in as little as 4 weeks.
faq.q4: Are EMCS credits the same as regular Ontario high school credits?
faq.a4: Yes. Our credits are identical to those earned at traditional Ontario day schools. They appear on official Ontario transcripts and are recognized by all Canadian colleges and universities.
faq.a5: Yes, all credit courses require a proctored final exam worth 30% of your grade. You can write your exam online with a remote proctor or at an approved testing center.
faq.q6: How do I get my marks sent to universities?
faq.a6: We send midterm and final marks directly to OUAC, OCAS, and your day school. Simply provide your application numbers through our transcript request system.
faq.q7: Can I transfer credits from another school?
faq.a7: Yes! We accept transfer credits from Ontario and other Canadian schools. International students can also have their transcripts evaluated for credit equivalency.
faq.q8: What is the difference between Self-Learning and Teacher-Led?
faq.a8: Self-Learning provides access to curriculum-aligned materials for practice—no assessments, no report card, no teacher interaction. Teacher-Led includes live online classes with Canadian teachers, required assessments, and an official Ontario academic report card.
faq.q9: Does the Self-Learning program provide credits?
faq.a9: No. The Primary Foundation Self-Learning program does not provide OSSD credits (which only apply to Grades 9-12) or an Ontario academic record. For an official record, either choose the Teacher-Led option or add the Ontario Academic Record add-on ($250 per course) to your Self-Learning enrollment.
faq.q10: Is the Primary Foundation program accredited?
faq.a10: EMCS is an Ontario Ministry-inspected private school. The Teacher-Led Primary Foundation program includes official Ontario academic report cards. The Self-Learning program provides curriculum-aligned practice materials without formal credentialing.
faq.q11: Do practice courses give me credits?
faq.a11: No. Practice courses are designed for skill-building and extra practice only. They do NOT earn Ontario high school credits and will NOT appear on any transcript or academic record. If you need credits, please enroll in our Credit Courses.
faq.a12: Video lessons, interactive practice activities, and homework exercises. No tests, no grades, no assessments, and no teacher instruction.
faq.q13: Do I need a study permit?
faq.a13: No. You can take EMCS online courses from anywhere in the world without a Canadian study permit.
faq.a14: Yes. The Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD) is recognized by universities worldwide, including institutions in Canada, the United States, United Kingdom, Australia, and many other countries.
faq.a15: You need a computer or tablet with internet access, a webcam for proctored exams, and basic software like a web browser and PDF reader. No special software purchases required.

cta.title: Still have questions?
cta.subtitle: Our team is here to help. Contact us and we'll get back to you within 24 hours.
